hero.map_desc: "On the frontier, respect is the only law. Between the bellows of the accordion and the barrel of the rifle, we forge our history."

bio.label: Brief Biography
bio.title: The Last Gaudério of the Frontier
bio.p1: Silvino Hermiro Jacques was born on February 17, 1906, in São Borja, RS. Son of Leão Pedro Jacques and Máxima de Sant'Anna, he grew up in a traditional frontier family.
bio.p2: He joined the Brigada Militar, but his life changed radically in 1929 after an incident in Santo Ângelo that made him a fugitive. Fleeing North, he served as a loyalist Capitão for his godfather, Getúlio Vargas, in the Revolution of 1932.
bio.p3: Silvino was a man of his word and poetry. His "Décimas" recorded his deeds. He protected families and fought political rivals, establishing a near-parallel state along the border zone between Brazil and Paraguay.
bio.p4: He fell in battle in May 1939 at the Aurora Farm. His story remains alive in oral memory as a folk hero, the last of the frontier gaudérios.
bio.caption: The Gaudério of the Frontier

vozes.title: Voices & Ecos
vozes.quote: If by chance one day I cannot claim victory, let my enemies kill me, and thus full of glory I ask my friends to read my history.

m1.mito_desc: Legend says that Silvino had a pact. Bullets would hit his chest and fall to the ground.
m1.fato_desc: Documents prove he was wounded several times. He survived due to military tactics and local protection.

m2.mito_desc: Accounts claim he buried revolution gold in secret locations along the border.
m2.fato_desc: There are no records of fortune. He died leaving very few material goods to his family.

m3.mito_desc: The popular belief that he stole only from the rich to give to the poor of the region.
m3.fato_desc: His actions were driven by local political disputes and clandestine survival along the frontier.
